title: Condensed Sourdough Recipe for Crusty Artisan Loaf
ingredients: 1 cup sourdough starter, bubbly and active
instructions: Feed your starter with ¾ cup warm water and 1 cup flour, aiming for thick pancake batter consistency.
At 1:00 p.m., take 1 cup starter out of your jar and feed it. Leave on the counter.
At 5:00 p.m., divide your starter into two bowls, 1 cup in each.
Add the rest of the ingredients for 1 recipe to each bowl and combine.
Cover bowls and let rest 30 minutes to 1 hour on the counter.
At 6:00 p.m., do your first set of folds by uncovering and pulling around the perimeter of your dough to the center, about 8 pulls. Cover.
At 7:00 p.m., do another set of pulls. Cover.
At 8:00 p.m., do another set of pulls. Cover and transfer to the fridge after your third pull if you have time, fitting in a few more pulls.

At 9:00 p.m., do another set of pulls. Place dough on parchment paper, pat/tuck/drag edges into a circular shape and place dough back inside the dirty bowl using the parchment paper to lift it. Cover.
Move both bowls to the fridge overnight.
In the morning (6:00 to 8:00 a.m.), take both bowls out of the fridge. They will sit on the counter for 1-2 hours.
Gently flour the tops of the loaves and score with kitchen scissors or bread lame.
Transfer bread to cast iron pan or baking pan with lid using the parchment.

Place lid on and put in the oven. Bake at 450°F for 45-50 minutes if putting directly into a cold oven, then remove the lid and bake 5 minutes further to brown. If oven is preheated, bake 35-40 minutes, remove the lid, and bake 5-10 minutes more to brown.
Transfer to a wire cooling rack and let cool at least 30 minutes before slicing.
notes: Over the next 5 hours after the first fold, aim to fit in 3-5 folds total
If you would like to add mixins (raisins, nuts, cheeses, spices), add them at 8:00 p.m. and they will be dispersed through the dough on future folds
Store cut side down on the counter for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 1 month
